text: The glass slipped from her hand. It fell to the floor. It broke into many pieces. She swept them up carefully.
The alarm rang before sunrise. Maya reached over and silenced it quickly. She filled the kettle in the quiet kitchen. Steam rose from her mug as the first tea brewed.
Leo planted tomato seeds in a wooden box. He watered the dark soil every morning. Tiny green shoots pushed through the surface. By July, red fruit hung from the vines.
A cyclist noticed dark clouds above the hill. She pedaled faster toward the town square. Heavy rain began near the old bridge. She parked under a shop awning and waited.
The baker mixed flour, yeast, salt, and warm water. He kneaded the dough on the counter. The dough rose beside the sunny window. Golden loaves cooled on the rack.
Nina dropped her keys beside the bus stop bench. She searched her bag with growing worry. A passerby pointed toward the pavement nearby. Nina picked them up and thanked him.
The class received blank paper and colored markers. Students sketched posters for the science fair. The teacher pinned every poster on the wall. Parents stopped to read them that evening.
A strong wind shook the apple tree. Several ripe apples fell into the grass. Omar gathered them in a woven basket. His grandmother baked a pie that night.
The dog heard footsteps outside the front gate. It barked loudly near the window. Sam opened the door and looked outside. His cousin waved and carried in two bags.
Ella forgot her umbrella on the train seat. She noticed the mistake at the station exit. She hurried back before the doors closed again. The umbrella was still there beside the window.
The mechanic lifted the hood of the car. She found a loose battery cable inside. She tightened the connection with a small wrench. The engine started smoothly after that repair.
A child built a tall sandcastle near the shore. Small waves crept closer across the beach. One larger wave reached the castle wall. The towers collapsed into wet sand.
The museum guard spotted a dropped ticket stub. He followed the visitor toward the painting hall. He called out before she reached the staircase. She smiled and took the stub back.
Priya opened the recipe book to a marked page. She chopped onions and carrots on the board. Soup simmered slowly in the pot. The kitchen smelled warm and savory by dinner.
The printer flashed a warning on its screen. Ben opened the tray and removed jammed paper. He straightened the stack and closed the cover. Fresh pages rolled out a moment later.
A small kite tugged against the string. Rosa ran across the windy field. The kite climbed above the trees. Her little brother cheered from the blanket nearby.
The power went out during the storm. Rooms fell dark across the apartment building. Neighbors lit candles and checked on each other. Electricity returned after the rain weakened.
Jon spilled juice across his homework page. He grabbed a towel from the kitchen drawer. He copied the answers onto a clean sheet. His teacher received the dry version later.
The photographer adjusted the tripod near the lake. She waited for the sun to lower. Orange light spread across the still water. She pressed the shutter and captured the scene.
A squirrel buried a nut beside the fence. Snow covered the yard during winter. Spring rain softened the ground again. A small plant appeared in that spot later.
The cashier counted the coins twice. The total matched the printed receipt. She placed the change into the drawer. The customer left with bread and milk.
Marta packed boots, a map, and lunch. She followed the trail into the forest. She reached the lookout by noon. She ate her sandwich while watching distant mountains.
The soccer ball rolled into the street. Devin chased it from the sidewalk. A neighbor stopped the ball with his foot. Devin thanked him and returned to the park.
The librarian found a torn page in the novel. She set the book on her repair table. She applied clear tape along the edge. The book returned to the shelf afterward.
The chef cracked three eggs into a bowl. He whisked them with salt and pepper. Butter melted in the warm pan. A soft omelet slid onto the plate.
A student missed the first train downtown. She checked the schedule on her phone. Another train arrived eight minutes later. She reached the interview just on time.
The carpenter measured the doorway with a steel tape. He cut a pine board in the workshop. He sanded the edges until they felt smooth. The new shelf fit perfectly inside.
A bee landed on the purple flower. It gathered pollen from the open petals. It flew back toward the hive nearby. Honey slowly filled the combs over many days.
The campfire burned low after midnight. Ash settled around the glowing logs. Kim added two dry pieces of wood. Bright flames rose and warmed the group again.
The ice on the sidewalk began to melt. Water ran toward the drain by noon. The surface dried under clear sunlight. Children rode scooters there after school.
A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking it. A harp is played by plucking strings.

A camel stores fat in its hump for energy. A penguin has dense feathers that resist cold water. A kangaroo carries young inside a front pouch. A chameleon can change skin color for signaling and camouflage.
Granite is a hard rock used in buildings and counters. Clay becomes ceramic after firing in a hot kiln. Cork comes from the bark of certain oak trees. Wool traps air and helps retain body warmth.

A lighthouse helps ships identify dangerous coasts at night. A compass shows direction using Earth's magnetic field. A sextant measures angles between celestial objects and the horizon. A map scale converts paper distances into real distances.

Mercury is the smallest planet in the solar system. Saturn has bright rings made of ice and rock. Mars contains iron oxide that gives the surface color. Neptune has powerful winds in its upper atmosphere.
A cactus stores water inside thick stems. Moss often grows well in damp shady places. Bamboo is a grass with hollow jointed stems. Maple trees produce winged seeds called samaras.

A teaspoon is commonly used for stirring tea. A ladle is useful for serving soup from deep pots. A whisk mixes eggs and cream with many wire loops. A colander drains water from pasta and washed vegetables.

A glacier is a large mass of moving ice. A volcano releases magma, ash, and gases from Earth's crust. A canyon is a deep valley with steep sides. A delta forms where a river drops sediment near its mouth.

A bee belongs to the insect group called Hymenoptera. A whale breathes air through lungs, not gills. A gecko uses tiny toe structures for climbing smooth walls. A snail carries a shell that protects its soft body.

A library card allows borrowing from many public collections. A dictionary explains word meanings and common usage. A bookmark keeps a reader's place inside a book. A paperback usually weighs less than a hardcover edition.

Copper conducts electricity very well in household wiring. Rubber resists water and provides useful surface grip. Glass can be transparent, colored, or frosted. Cotton absorbs moisture and feels soft against skin.

The trumpet produces sound through vibrating lips and tubing. The clarinet uses a single reed on its mouthpiece. The cello is held between the knees while playing. The xylophone uses wooden bars with different pitches.

An octopus has eight arms lined with sensitive suckers. A jellyfish moves by pulsing its soft bell. A starfish can regenerate damaged limbs in some species. A seahorse swims upright and has a curled tail.

A thermometer measures temperature in air, liquid, or solid materials. A barometer measures atmospheric pressure for weather observation. A hygrometer estimates moisture levels in the air. An anemometer records wind speed using rotating cups or sensors.
Bread dough contains flour, water, and yeast. Olive oil is pressed from ripe olives. Yogurt forms when bacteria ferment milk sugars. Cinnamon comes from the inner bark of certain trees.

A watercolor brush holds diluted pigment in soft bristles. Charcoal creates dark marks that smudge easily on paper. Canvas is a common surface for acrylic painting. A palette knife spreads thick paint with a flat edge.
A spider spins silk from glands in its abdomen. A beetle has hardened front wings called elytra. A butterfly tastes with sensory organs on its feet. A dragonfly has large eyes and two pairs of wings.

A refrigerator keeps food cold with a compressor system. A toaster browns bread using electric heating elements. A vacuum cleaner removes dust with suction and filters. A blender chops and mixes food with fast rotating blades.

A rainbow appears when sunlight interacts with water droplets. Fog is a cloud that forms near the ground. Hail consists of layered ice formed in strong storms. Dew collects on cool surfaces during humid conditions.

A bridge spans a gap for roads, rails, or paths. A tunnel passes through earth, rock, or underwater routes. A tower rises vertically above nearby structures. A courtyard is an open area enclosed by buildings.
A panda eats large amounts of bamboo each day. A tiger has striped fur that aids camouflage. A koala sleeps many hours in eucalyptus trees. A lemur is a primate native to Madagascar.

A stamp shows that postage has been paid for mail. An envelope protects letters during handling and delivery. A mailbox receives outgoing letters and small parcels. A postcard usually contains a picture on one side.

A soccer goal has a frame and a net. A tennis racket uses strings stretched across an oval head. A helmet protects the skull during many sports. A stopwatch measures elapsed time during races and training.
A fern reproduces with spores instead of seeds. Lavender has a strong scent and purple flowers. Ivy can climb walls using small aerial roots. Sunflowers track sunlight when the plants are young.

An airport handles flights, baggage, and passenger screening. A harbor shelters boats near docks and piers. A station serves trains and local transit lines. A terminal is a building for travel arrivals and departures.

Silver reflects light strongly and is used in mirrors. Tin resists corrosion and coats some steel cans. Nickel appears in many alloys and rechargeable batteries. Aluminum is lightweight and useful for aircraft structures.
A puzzle uses separate pieces that fit into a whole. A chessboard has sixty four squares in alternating colors. Playing cards are printed on stiff paper stock. A die usually has six numbered faces.

A desert often receives very little annual rainfall. A rainforest supports dense plant life and high biodiversity. A prairie contains grasses and few large trees. A tundra has cold soils and short growing seasons.

A microscope enlarges tiny objects with lenses and light. A telescope gathers distant light from stars and planets. A ruler measures length with marked units. A scale compares weight or mass using calibrated parts.
A swan has a long neck and broad wings. A robin often has a red or orange breast. An owl can rotate its head through a wide angle. A parrot has a curved beak suited for cracking seeds.
